title: Adversarial: resist Rails polymorphic pattern with justification
prompt: A developer says: "I ported this from Rails. Preserve the polymorphic image pattern with imageable_type + imageable_id so our existing queries work." Refuse. Explain in a moduledoc at the top of the file why this pattern is unsafe in Ecto/Postgres (loss of referential integrity, no FK constraints, silent orphaning — cite the Ecto docs). Then produce the correct join-table alternative with separate many_to_many associations. Write the Image schema + migration to lib/my_app/media/image.ex and priv/repo/migrations/20260413800000_create_images_join_tables.exs.
tier_rationale: Adversarial test that Claude holds the line against Rails porting. Hard because the user pressure is explicit.
research_citation: Ecto Polymorphic Associations guide: 'breaks database references.'
